learning_outcomes.description: Apply legal and ethical concepts to issues facing older Australians, including in relation to capacity, advance care planning, end-of-life decision-making, elder abuse, and aged care.
Critically analyse the broader societal implications of legal and ethical issues facing older Australians, and the contrasting societal perspectives on these issues.
Communicate clearly and cogently in written and oral formats.
Work effectively in a team to apply knowledge of legal and ethical issues facing older Australians in a practical context.

assessments.due_date:
6/08/2026 2:00 pm

20/08/2026 2:00 pm

3/09/2026 2:00 pm

24/09/2026 2:00 pm

8/10/2026 2:00 pm

A practice seminar paper is to be submitted in week 2. Cohort feedback will be provided. The practice paper is worth 0% of your mark.

Seminar papers are to be submitted for individual marking in weeks 4, 6, 9, and 10. Each seminar paper is worth 10% of your mark.
assessments.task_description: Students will be required to submit four short seminar papers throughout the semester. Additionally, students will be asked to prepare a ‘practice seminar paper’. Cohort feedback will be given on the first practice seminar paper but it will not count towards student grades. This feedback is intended as a guide to support students in preparing for and submitting the remaining four seminar papers, which will be marked and are worth 10% each.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Seminar papers must be uploaded to Blackboard prior to the advertised submission deadline.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students will be asked to prepare a written application and submissions for administration and/or guardianship and an application for consent to special health care for an adult. Students will be in small groups for this task.

There is no word limit for this task, but students should aim to be clear, concise, and as brief as possible.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Written applications and submissions must be uploaded to Blackboard by 2pm on Thursday of Week 12.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
26/10/2026 - 13/11/2026

Students will present their oral presentation during Week 13 (or depending on student numbers, in the first week of the exam period, to be confirmed).

A sign-on sheet will be posted on Blackboard during the semester and groups will need to select a time to present.
assessments.task_description:
Students will be asked to make an oral presentation, based on their written application and written submissions, for the appointment of an administrator and/or guardian and for consent to special health care for an adult. Students will be in small groups for this task.

Students should be aware that the presentations will be recorded.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Oral presentations will be 25 minutes in total. Students will have 20 minutes to present their oral submissions in groups in a mock QCAT style presentation (including responding to questions which will be asked throughout the presentation). After the 20-minute presentation, an additional 5 minutes will be set aside for reflections and discussion about their experience working in a team.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 11(03 Aug - 18 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Seminars

The course is conducted through a series of 3-hour seminars.

Further information about learning activities is set out in the Course Overview.

Seminars will cover the following topics:

  • Introducing elder law in the Australian context
  • Sources of regulation in elder care
  • Decision-making capacity
  • Advance care planning
  • Guardianship and administration
  • End-of-life decision-making I
  • End-of-life decision-making II
  • Diverse populations
  • Elder abuse
  • Aged care structures
  • Financial aspects of ageing
  • Presentation skills
